_comment_url: One URL per TEMPLATE, not per page. Three of these were added when the site grew
past the original three: a collection-driven hub, an expert profile and a
hand-built support page were all shipping unmeasured. Adding a page to an existing
template does not need a new entry here; adding a new template does.
hub -> owner-builder-courses (HubLayout via a stub route)
cpd -> cpd (hand-built, converts to a hub in B3; drop this then)
styleguide -> the component gallery, heaviest page in the build

_comment_styleguide: The component gallery, held to its own LCP budget. It renders all 34 components
on one page, is noindex and excluded from the sitemap, and is never served to a
reader: it is the heaviest page in the build BY DESIGN and representative of no
real page. At 1800ms it sat exactly on the CI runner's boundary and flipped at
random - measured 1804, 1805, 1806 and 1807 on failing runs and under 1800 on
passing ones, with no code change between them. Locally the same build measures
1506-1512ms across five runs, so the runner is ~300ms slower than a dev machine.
A gate that fails a coin flip blocks merges without ever catching a defect.
2200ms keeps it a real check (a genuine regression here would be far larger than
400ms) while leaving the five production URLs strict at 1800. Every other
assertion below is unchanged, including minScore 1.
